expectations.evidence: Erfpacht is a limited right in rem (beperkt zakelijk recht) under Art. 5:85 BW, distinct from ordinary rental (huur). It entitles the holder (erfpachter) to hold and use immovable property belonging to another — in this case, the Gemeente Amsterdam. ... The canon is the periodic payment made by the erfpachter to the grondeigenaar (here: the Gemeente Amsterdam) in exchange for the use of the land (Art. 5:89 BW).

Amsterdam is unique in the Netherlands: the Gemeente Amsterdam owns approximately 80% of all land within the city, and virtually all residential and commercial property is on erfpacht land. Amsterdam has operated a municipal erfpacht system since 1896. ... The Amsterdam Erfpacht System — Critical Context. ... AB2016 — Eeuwigdurende Erfpacht (The Reformed Amsterdam System). Since 1 July 2016, the Gemeente Amsterdam issues all new erfpacht under the AB2016 (eeuwigdurende erfpacht) conditions.

The most significant financial risk for any buyer is the canonherziening — the periodic revision of the canon when the applicable revision moment arrives. ... Given that Amsterdam property values have increased dramatically over the past 20+ years, a canon that has not been revised in 20 years is almost certainly well below what it would be after revision. The canonsprong (canon shock/leap) at the next revision moment can be enormous: Example: An erfpachter paying EUR 500/year in canon may face EUR 5,000–10,000/year or more after revision.

Transfer of the apartment (the appartementsrecht on erfpacht land) requires: A notariele akte van levering (notarial deed of transfer) — mandatory under Art. 3:89 BW. Registration at the kadaster. The notaris must verify: The erfpacht conditions (erfpachtvoorwaarden) and applicable AB; Any canon arrears... Engage a notaris with Amsterdam erfpacht expertise — not all notarissen have deep familiarity with the Amsterdam system.
